bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no need to concede yet, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueC, which is valid but gives me very low utility, and their message is only pressure rather than evidence of a real constraint.

I am targeting a very high utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02) and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the same all-valueC offer that gives me very low utility. Their messages are aggressive cheap talk and are not supported by meaningful movement in the offer history, but I will make a small concession on a low-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my prior bid.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the exact all-valueC offer and used pressure language, but their bids show no concession; I will concede slightly on low-weight issues while holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making visible concessions on my lowest-weight issues. The opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering all valueC, and their messages are aggressive cheap talk rather than evidence of new constraints. I will preserve valueA on the highest-weight issues A, B, and C, while moving the lower-weight D, E, and F to valueB to avoid repeating my last bid and show movement.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.76 by holding firm on my highest-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding the lower-weight issues D, E, and F. Since it is still very early at t=0.08, I should not make major concessions on core issues; the opponent has only slightly moved on issueB while their messages remain aggressive cheap talk, so I am responding to the bidding signal rather than the rhetoric.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.68 by holding firm on the highest-weight issues A and C while conceding more on issueB and the low-weight issues D/E/F. At t=0.10 it is still early, but the opponent has repeatedly held near valueC and only moved to valueB on issueB, so I use that concrete concession signal rather than their aggressive messages.

At t=0.12 it is still early, but the opponent has held firm on most issues and only signaled movement on issueB from valueC to valueB, so I am targeting a still-strong utility while making a visible concession. I will keep my highest-weight issueA at valueA, accept valueB on issueB, and make one additional concession on issueC to valueB while leaving the already-conceded low-weight issues at valueC.

I am targeting about 0.52 utility, which is a meaningful concession from my last offer while still protecting my highest-weight issueA this early at t=0.14. The opponent has begun conceding slightly from all valueC offers, but their messages are mostly pressure tactics; their actual bids still remain quite unfavorable to me, so I will move on issueC rather than give up issueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.56, preserving my most important issueA while still leaving several lower-weight issues at valueC. Time is still early at t=0.16, and the opponent’s actual offers show only small, inconsistent concessions despite aggressive messages, so I should not make a major drop yet. Their history suggests they may tolerate valueB on issueB and issueD, so I incorporate those signals while avoiding an exact repeat of my prior bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.56, keeping firm on high-weight issueA while making concessions on lower or more recently signaled issues. At t=0.18 it is still early enough not to make major concessions, but the opponent has repeatedly held near valueC and recently moved issueC to valueB, so I am incorporating that signal while avoiding a repeat of my last bid. Their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their offers show only limited concession so far.

I am targeting about 0.60 utility: still favorable to me, but with substantial concessions on lower-weight issues and issueC. At t=0.20 there is no need to concede my highest-weight issueA yet, despite the opponent’s pressure; their actual bids show some movement on issueB but they remain very demanding elsewhere.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64, which is still strong for me but makes a visible concession on the most important disputed issue, issueA. Since it is still early at t=0.22, I should not concede too deeply; the opponent’s actual offers show only limited movement, mainly on issueB, despite aggressive messages asking for more.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.60: still well above the opponent’s latest offer to me, but with meaningful concessions on issueB, issueC, and the lower-weight issues. Since time is only at 0.24, I should not collapse to their position yet, but their offers show some movement, especially the latest concession on issueA, so I am preserving issueA while softening elsewhere. Their messages are mostly pressure tactics, so I rely more on the offer history than the accusations of lowballing.

I am targeting about 0.56 utility for myself, which is a real concession from my recent offers while still protecting the most important issueA. The opponent’s bids are inconsistent rather than steadily improving for me—their last offer is much worse for me than their prior issueA/valueA concession—so at t=0.26 I will not make a drastic move, but I will incorporate their latest movement on issueF.

I am targeting a utility around 0.56, a moderate concession from my earlier high-utility offers while still preserving value on important issues. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low-value proposals, but their latest offer shows some movement on issueA and issueC, and their past offers suggest issueB may be easier for them to concede. Their messages are aggressive but not binding; I am relying on the actual bidding pattern instead.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.53: lower than my last offer but still above the opponent’s best offers to me so far. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure to keep conceding, but not enough to collapse to the opponent’s near-all-valueC position. The opponent’s bids show willingness to move on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueE at different times, while their aggressive messages are cheap talk, so I am combining some signaled concessions without giving up all high-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.56, modestly above the opponent’s latest offer to me while still making substantial concessions on several issues. At t=0.32 there is no need to collapse to their exact bid yet, but their offers show gradual movement, especially on issueA and issueB, so I am preserving those gains while asking for a small improvement on low-weight issueE. Their repeated “lowball” messages are treated as pressure tactics; the actual bid history is the useful signal.

I am targeting roughly the same utility as my last offer (~0.56) while reshuffling concessions to align better with the opponent’s latest signals. At t=0.34 there is time to hold value on the high-weight issueA while conceding low-weight issueE and accepting their recent movement on issueF=valueB; their messages are aggressive, but the actual offers show only limited concession.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.53: still well above reservation, but a real concession from my prior 0.56-level offers. At t=0.36 there is no need to collapse, yet the opponent has stayed aggressive and is signaling pressure on issueA, so I concede issueA to valueB while preserving valueB on the more important issueB/issueC rather than dropping everything to valueC.

I am targeting a mid-game utility a little above 0.5, conceding further on lower-priority issues while keeping some value on the high-weight issueA. The opponent’s messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their bids show willingness to give valueA on issueB and valueB on issueE while still demanding many valueC outcomes, so I am responding with a concrete compromise that improves on their latest offer for me without repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a mid-game utility of about 0.49, conceding meaningfully from my earlier higher-utility bids while still protecting the most important issueA at valueB rather than valueC. The opponent’s offers show only intermittent concessions and their messages are mostly pressure/lowball framing, so I trust the bid history more than the rhetoric and move on lower-confidence tradeoffs without dropping to their latest low-utility proposal.
